content.text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 13:47 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix for this issue on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push it to the local `origin`.

Keep the review branch limited to the Shape Sort hotfix, preserve unrelated local work while preparing it, and restore that local work after the push.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git status && git log --oneline -8 && git branch -a

content.text:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

Good — I can see the problem clearly. The tiles are 72×72px with only 6px drag padding, which is too small for touch targets (Apple/Google guidelines recommend at least 44–48px minimum, and the tiles themselves should be larger on mobile). Now let me set up the hotfix branch cleanly.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git stash push -u -m "wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix" && git stash list

content.text: Now apply the fixes — larger tiles, bigger drag padding, and a `touch-action` CSS property to prevent scroll-hijack on mobile:

arguments.command:
cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git add public/games/shape-sort/style.css public/games/shape-sort/game.js && git commit -m "fix(shape-sort): enlarge touch targets and drag hit area on mobile

Tiles were 72×72 px with only 6 px drag padding — too small for reliable
finger interaction on phones.

Changes:
- style.css: add @media (pointer: coarse) block that bumps tiles to
  96×96 px (border-radius scaled to match) and sets touch-action: none
  to prevent scroll interference during drag.
- game.js: add isCoarsePointer() helper; canGrabTile() now uses
  mobileDragPaddingPx (20 px) on touch devices instead of the desktop
  dragPaddingPx (6 px), keeping desktop behaviour unchanged.

No changes to game logic, snap tolerance, or unrelated files."

content.text:
Everything's back exactly as it was. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

**What changed (Shape Sort only — 2 files, 1 commit)**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `style.css` | Added `@media (pointer: coarse)` block: tiles grow from 72→96 px, `border-radius` scaled proportionally, `touch-action: none` prevents scroll from stealing the drag gesture |
| `game.js` | Added `isCoarsePointer()` helper; `canGrabTile()` now uses `mobileDragPaddingPx: 20` on touch devices vs. the existing `dragPaddingPx: 6` on desktop — desktop behaviour is untouched |

**Why these numbers:**
- 96 px tiles comfortably exceed the 44 px minimum touch-target guideline (Apple HIG / Material Design)
- 20 px drag padding gives ~136 px effective hit area on a 96 px tile — forgiving for small fingers
- `pointer: coarse` is the right media query here (targets touchscreens without breaking mouse users on small viewports)

**Branch:** `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` — pushed to `origin`, branched from `main`, contains exactly one commit scoped to this fix.

**Local work:** stashed before branching, restored to `main` after push — `README.md`, `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md` deletion, `notes/homepage-copy-review.md`, `.env`, and `playtest/` are all back untouched.
